puzzle.title: The Throne of Truth
puzzle.intro: A powerful ruler, known for their great wisdom and magnificent throne, receives a mysterious message from an unknown source. This unexpected communication challenges their beliefs and authority, setting them on a diplomatic journey that will test their intellect and ultimately lead to a profound spiritual awakening. Can you uncover the identity of this sovereign and the events that transformed their entire kingdom?
clues: My people and I worshipped the sun, following the ways of our ancestors.
A bird, a hoopoe, was the unlikely messenger that brought me a letter from a powerful leader.
To test the sender's wisdom, I sent valuable gifts, but they were returned, as the sender possessed far greater wealth from Allah.
Before I even arrived at the sender's court, my magnificent throne was miraculously transported there in the blink of an eye.
Upon my arrival, I was asked if the transported throne was mine, and I answered, 'It is as though it is the very one.'
I entered a great hall with a floor I mistook for a pool of water, and in that moment of awe, I submitted myself alongside Prophet Sulaiman to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.

trial.label: The Central Event
options: Receiving a letter from Prophet Sulaiman
Building an ark to survive a flood
trial.answer: Receiving a letter from Prophet Sulaiman

location.label: The Key Object
options: A staff that turned into a serpent
A ship made of planks and nails

outcome.label: The Ultimate Outcome
options: Submitting to the one true God (Allah)
Being turned into a pillar of salt
Building a high tower to reach God
Being swallowed by a great fish
Losing immense wealth as a punishment
outcome.answer: Submitting to the one true God (Allah)

puzzle.arabic: قِيلَ لَهَا ٱدْخُلِى ٱلصَّرْحَ ۖ فَلَمَّا رَأَتْهُ حَسِبَتْهُ لُجَّةًۭ وَكَشَفَتْ عَن سَاقَيْهَا ۚ قَالَ إِنَّهُۥ صَرْحٌۭ مُّمَرَّدٌۭ مِّن قَوَارِيرَ ۗ قَالَتْ رَبِّ إِنِّى ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِى وَأَسْلَمْتُ مَعَ سُلَيْمَـٰنَ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ
puzzle.verse: She was told, "Enter the palace." But when she saw it, she thought it was a body of water and uncovered her shins [to wade through]. He said, "Indeed, it is a palace [whose floor is] made smooth with glass." She said, "My Lord, indeed I have wronged myself, and I submit with Solomon to Allāh, Lord of the worlds." (27:44)
